reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails to strictly follow the content constraints of the prompt. Instead of simply saying the target sentence '每个字，都要读清楚', it generates a conversational response: '好的，我会用极慢速，每个字都读清楚，你等着，哦……' (Okay, I will use extremely slow speed, read every word clearly, you wait, oh...). While the model attempts to sound slow by inserting long pauses between its conversational filler phrases (e.g., 00:04-00:06), the actual execution of the target sentence (00:07-00:09) is spoken at a moderate, near-normal speed (approx. 4 characters/second), not 'extremely slow'. The 'slowness' is simulated through hesitation and added text rather than slowing down the articulation of the required phonemes.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 follows the content instructions perfectly, reciting only the target sentence: '每个字，都要读清楚'. Regarding the '语速' (speed) dimension, the model effectively implements the 'extremely slow' requirement. The phrase is stretched over approximately 3.5 seconds for just 8 characters. It includes a deliberate pause between '每个字' (00:00-00:01) and '都要读清楚' (00:02-00:03), and the articulation of each character is prolonged and distinct, satisfying the requirement to 'read clearly'. The prosody is natural for a slow-reading task.
model_comparison: Model 2 demonstrates superior performance in both content accuracy and the target paralinguistic feature. Model 1 hallucinates conversational filler ('好的...', '你等着...'), violating the command to simply 'say' the text. Furthermore, Model 1 fails to apply the 'extremely slow' speed to the actual target sentence, which is spoken at a relatively normal pace amidst the filler. Model 2 accurately isolates the target text and applies a consistent, deliberate, and slow reading pace that fits the 'extremely slow' description well. Model 2 is the clear winner.
